tips: Use --skill <name> to load a specific documentation persona skill
Run --dry-run (-d) to preview changes without writing files
Install community skills with --install <name> or --install all
List installed skills and model providers with --list (-l)
Switch models anytime with --model <name> (-m)
Override context window with --ctx <number> (-c)
Persistent project state is saved to docs/.neorwc
Init global config folder with --init (-n)
Browse remote templates with --templates (-t)
Set NEORWC_GOOGLE_KEY env var or save it via --config for Google provider
Global config lives at ~/.config/neostore/neorwc/config.json
Edit ignore patterns inside the --config TUI when prompted
Use --provider to switch between google, openai, and ollama
Provider and model choices persist across runs via global config
API keys saved via --config are reused across all your projects
The TUI sidebar shows current provider and model at a glance
Models are sorted by context window size — largest first
Context window is auto-detected from modelpedia metadata
Press C-c at any time to exit the TUI without saving
Ignore patterns support glob syntax like **/node_modules/**
Use neorwc --init to create the global config folder with starter skills
The --list flag shows both local skills and remote model providers
Documentation is generated into the docs/ folder by default
Each generated doc file ends with 'written by Neorwc'
Skills are markdown files loaded from ~/.config/neostore/neorwc/skills/
Ollama runs models locally — no internet or API key required
Google Gemini models have a 1M+ token context window
OpenAI models support function calling and structured outputs
The --dry-run flag shows what files would be created without writing them
Template files are fetched from github.com/rkriad585/neorwc-templates
Run neorwc --config to launch the interactive configuration TUI
The TUI sidebar highlights your current provider and model selection
Context window is auto-detected from model metadata — you never set it manually
Edit ignore patterns when prompted inside the TUI config flow
API keys are stored in global config, reusable across all your projects
Provider and model choices persist across runs in global config.json
The Esc key goes back one step in the TUI workflow
Press C-c anytime to exit the TUI cleanly without saving
Models in the TUI are sorted by context window size, largest first
Google Gemini models offer 1M+ token context windows
OpenAI models support function calling and structured JSON output
Ollama runs entirely locally — no API key or internet needed
The --list flag shows both installed skills and available providers
Skills are plain markdown files loaded from ~/.config/neostore/neorwc/skills/
Each generated doc ends with 'written by Neorwc'
Use --init to create the global config folder with starter skills
Ignore patterns support glob syntax: **/node_modules/**, *.log, etc.
